response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a versatile full-stack engineer with genuine strengths in distributed systems and testing culture, but he critically fails to meet the primary technical prerequisite of 8 years of C++ experience — his resume shows roughly 6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis project, totaling under a year. His primary stack is TypeScript, DLang, and Node.js. Beyond the C++ gap, he has no demonstrated ownership of a cloud compute platform, no formal technical leadership of engineering teams, and his infrastructure exposure — while real — is at the platform-consumer level (AWS CDK, Kubernetes deployment) rather than the platform-builder level this role demands.
strengths: Genuine distributed systems exposure at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency DLang services
Strong testing and engineering-practice culture demonstrated consistently across roles and through public workshops, Selenium courses, and TDD tooling
Staff-level breadth across full-stack, DevOps tooling, and AI-agent infrastructure showing capacity to contribute across organizational boundaries
concerns: C++ experience is effectively under one year, versus the 8-year hard minimum — this alone is disqualifying
No evidence of owning or architecting a cloud compute platform; cloud exposure is limited to consuming AWS CDK and deploying on Kubernetes
No demonstrated technical leadership of an engineering team at the scale and ambiguity the role requires; mentoring has been workshop/community-level, not org-level
